title: CompViz: A Dynamic Benchmark for Compositional Visual Reasoning with Sub-100ms Generation
description: We propose CompViz, a procedurally-generated benchmark targeting sub-100ms generation speed to enable dynamic evaluation paradigms infeasible with slower benchmarks. Unlike MM-CondChain (hours per batch), MentisOculi (minutes per instance), and DynaMath (seconds per handcrafted seed), CompViz achieves target throughput of >10,000 instances/hour through optimized 2D SVG generation, enabling per-run contamination-free evaluation and dynamic difficulty adjustment. CompViz specifically targets nested quantification (∃∀, ∀∃) and transitive relational reasoning—distinct from MM-CondChain's conditional control flow focus and DynaMath's mathematical reasoning domain—using 2D abstract patterns with fine-grained difficulty control through composition depth.
motivation: Current procedural benchmarks suffer from a critical limitation: slow generation. MM-CondChain requires hours for batch generation via agentic synthesis; MentisOculi requires minutes per mental imagery instance; InfiniBench requires ~5 min/frame for 3D rendering; DynaMath uses handcrafted seed programs with sophisticated rendering. This speed constraint prevents dynamic evaluation paradigms: per-run fresh test sets for contamination-free evaluation and real-time difficulty adjustment. Existing static benchmarks (CLEVR, CVR) are saturated or limited in reasoning diversity. Critically, NO existing benchmark targets nested quantification (∃∀, ∀∃) and transitive relational chains as core challenges. CompViz fills both gaps: FAST (<100ms target) generation enabling dynamic capabilities, with unique focus on logical/quantitative reasoning with nested quantifiers.
proposed_approach: CompViz generates visual reasoning tasks through an optimized three-stage pipeline: (1) Scene Generation: 2D SVG scenes with shapes, colors, textures, and spatial relations (target: <50ms); (2) Query Generation: Sampling from a compositional grammar including Boolean operators, quantifiers (∃, ∀, exactly-n), comparisons, and transitive relations (target: <30ms); (3) Answer Computation: Symbolic execution on ground-truth scene graphs (target: <20ms). Total target generation: <100ms/instance. Fine-grained difficulty scales via composition depth (1-4+), object count (3-20), distractors, and quantifier nesting. Enables: (a) Per-run contamination-free evaluation with fresh test sets; (b) Dynamic difficulty adjustment based on model performance; (c) Demonstration of curriculum learning feasibility (generation of 20K instances in <15 min). Explicitly addresses query naturalness through human comprehension validation and alternative formulations.
key_innovations: Sub-100ms target generation enabling dynamic evaluation paradigms (contamination-free per-run evaluation, dynamic difficulty adjustment) infeasible with MM-CondChain's hour-long generation
Unique reasoning focus: nested quantifiers (∃∀, ∀∃) and transitive relation chains distinct from MM-CondChain's conditional control flow AND DynaMath's mathematical reasoning
Explicit differentiation from DynaMath: logical/quantitative reasoning vs. math reasoning; compositional grammar (unlimited) vs. 501 handcrafted seeds
Query naturalness validation: human comprehension pilot and syntactic complexity controls to address nested quantification linguistic challenges
Fine-grained difficulty control through explicitly quantified composition depth and quantifier nesting
Complementary failure mode analysis: logical/quantitative errors vs. conditional/branching (MM-CondChain) vs. mathematical (DynaMath) errors
Open-source release with generation pipeline failure mode documentation and mitigations

related_work.paper: MM-CondChain: A Programmatically Verified Benchmark for Visually Grounded Deep Compositional Reasoning (Shen et al., arXiv 2026)
related_work.authors: Haozhan Shen, Shilin Yan, Hongwei Xue, et al.
related_work.difference: CONCURRENT WORK (March 2026). MM-CondChain tests conditional control flow in natural images/charts/GUIs using agentic VPIR synthesis (hours per batch). CompViz targets 10,000× faster generation (<100ms), enabling dynamic evaluation. MM-CondChain focuses on if-then-else branching; CompViz focuses on nested quantification and transitive relations. Complementary: MM-CondChain for comprehensive multi-domain eval, CompViz for rapid dynamic/contamination-free eval.

related_work.paper: MentisOculi: Revealing the Limits of Reasoning with Mental Imagery (Wiedemer et al., arXiv 2026)
related_work.authors: Thaddäus Wiedemer, Fanfei Li, Thomas Klein, et al.
related_work.difference: CONCURRENT WORK (February 2026). MentisOculi tests mental imagery and visual state maintenance (Form Board, Rush Hour, Paper Fold). CompViz tests direct visual reasoning with natural language queries. MentisOculi requires minutes per instance; CompViz targets <100ms.

related_work.paper: DynaMath: A Dynamic Visual Benchmark for Evaluating Mathematical Reasoning Robustness of Vision Language Models (Zou et al., arXiv 2024)
related_work.authors: Chengke Zou, Xingang Guo, Rui Yang, et al.
related_work.difference: Related work (October 2024). DynaMath uses 501 handcrafted seed programs for mathematical reasoning (geometry, algebra, arithmetic). CompViz uses compositional grammar for unlimited logical/quantitative reasoning with nested quantifiers. DynaMath tests math problem-solving; CompViz tests nested quantification and transitive relations. Distinct but complementary domains.

related_work.paper: CLEVR: A Diagnostic Dataset for Compositional Language and Elementary Visual Reasoning (Johnson et al., CVPR 2017)
related_work.authors: Justin Johnson, Bharath Hariharan, Laurens van der Maaten, et al.
related_work.difference: CLEVR uses fixed 3D primitives and has been saturated (>97% accuracy). CompViz offers diverse 2D patterns with fine-grained difficulty control through nested quantifiers and transitive relations not in CLEVR.

related_work.paper: CVR: A Benchmark for Compositional Visual Reasoning (Zerroug et al., NeurIPS 2022)
related_work.authors: Aimen Zerroug, Maulik Vaishnav, Jenelle Feather, et al.
related_work.difference: CVR focuses narrowly on odd-one-out tasks with binary classification. CompViz covers broader reasoning types with natural language queries including nested quantification. Includes direct comparison experiments.

related_work.paper: InfiniBench: Infinite Benchmarking for Visual Spatial Reasoning (Wang et al., arXiv 2025)
related_work.authors: Haoming Wang, Qiyao Xue, Wei Gao
related_work.difference: InfiniBench generates photorealistic 3D scenes for spatial reasoning (~5 min/frame). CompViz generates 2D patterns targeting <100ms for logical/quantitative reasoning, not spatial reasoning.

related_work.paper: DyVal: Dynamic Evaluation of Large Language Models for Reasoning Tasks (Zhu et al., ICLR 2024)
related_work.authors: Kaijie Zhu, Jindong Wang, Qinlin Zhao, et al.
related_work.difference: DyVal uses DAG-based dynamic evaluation for LLM reasoning (text-only). CompViz extends dynamic evaluation to vision-language models with visual scene generation.

related_work.paper: LiveBench: A Challenging, Contamination-Limited LLM Benchmark (White et al., ICLR 2025)
related_work.authors: Colin White, Samuel Dooley, Micah Goldblum, et al.
related_work.difference: LiveBench uses temporally-fresh data for contamination-free LLM evaluation. CompViz achieves contamination-free evaluation through procedural generation (fresh test sets per run) for VLMs.

related_work.paper: Explain Before You Answer: A Survey on Compositional Visual Reasoning (Ke et al., arXiv 2025)
related_work.authors: Fucai Ke, Joy Hsu, Zhixi Cai, et al.
related_work.difference: This survey identifies the gap CompViz fills: 'existing benchmarks lack explicit quantification of reasoning difficulty levels' and calls for benchmarks enabling 'systematic evaluation across question complexities.'

hypothesis: A benchmark targeting sub-100ms generation enabling dynamic evaluation paradigms, with unique focus on nested quantification (∃∀, ∀∃) and transitive relation chains distinct from conditional control flow (MM-CondChain) and mathematical reasoning (DynaMath), will: (1) Achieve <100ms generation (mean target) enabling practical contamination-free per-run evaluation; (2) Reveal specific failures on nested quantification and transitive reasoning invisible to existing benchmarks; (3) Show >30% accuracy gaps between difficulty levels demonstrating discriminative power; (4) Exhibit complementary failure modes to MM-CondChain (logical/quantitative vs. conditional/branching) and DynaMath (logical vs. mathematical); (5) Demonstrate <10% query naturalness confound through validation experiments.

confirming: Generation time validated <100ms with σ<20ms across all difficulty levels
>30% accuracy gap between Level 1 and Level 4 difficulty
Nested quantification (∃∀) shows >25% accuracy drop vs simple existential
Transitive relation accuracy degrades predictably with chain length
Query naturalness validation shows <10% parsing vs reasoning confound
Per-run evaluation variance <3% across 5 fresh test sets
Failure modes distinct from MM-CondChain and DynaMath
Open-source release with documented generation pipeline
refuting: All models achieve >90% on hardest difficulty (benchmark too easy)
No performance difference between reasoning types
Perfect correlation with existing benchmarks (no new signal)

timing_validation.description: Engineering validation measuring generation time across difficulty levels

test_sets.fixed: 2,000 instances per difficulty level for comparison studies
test_sets.dynamic: Fresh 1,000 instances per evaluation run for contamination-free study

experiments.name: Speed Validation
experiments.description: Measure generation time across difficulty levels with variance statistics

experiments.name: Discriminative Power
experiments.description: Evaluate model accuracy across difficulty levels (Level 1-4)

experiments.name: Nested Quantification Analysis
experiments.description: Compare ∃, ∃∀, ∀∃ patterns to isolate quantifier nesting difficulty

experiments.name: Transitive Relation Scaling
experiments.description: Measure accuracy vs relation chain length (1-hop, 2-hop, 3-hop)

experiments.name: Query Naturalness Validation
experiments.description: Compare performance on complex vs simplified query formulations

experiments.name: Contamination-Free Evaluation
experiments.description: 5 runs with fresh test sets, measure variance

experiments.name: CVR Comparison
experiments.description: Implement CVR tasks, measure correlation and incremental signal

experiments.name: Failure Mode Analysis
experiments.description: Expert analysis of MM-CondChain and DynaMath examples vs CompViz

experiments.name: Curriculum Learning Feasibility
experiments.description: Demonstrate 20K instance generation in <15 min (reduced scope)

metrics: Accuracy by task type and difficulty level
Transitive chain accuracy by hop count
Query naturalness performance gap (complex vs simplified)
Generation time with variance (mean, std, P95, P99)

computational_requirements.time: ~5 hours for full evaluation suite (within 8-hour budget)

feasibility: Highly feasible within constraints: (1) 2D SVG generation is CPU-lightweight with well-established libraries (cairosvg); (2) No training required—zero-shot evaluation only; (3) Open-source models (4-7B) fit on A6000; (4) Curriculum learning demonstration reduced to feasibility validation (generation only, not full training); (5) Complementary positioning to MM-CondChain and DynaMath reduces novelty risk—explicitly different reasoning focus and use case.
timing_note: The <100ms generation time is an ENGINEERING TARGET based on comparable SVG generation benchmarks, NOT a pre-validated measurement. Validation is part of the experimental plan.

enabled_capabilities: Per-run contamination-free evaluation with fresh test sets
Curriculum learning data generation at scale

from_mm_condchain.reasoning_type: Nested quantification/transitive relations vs conditional control flow
from_mm_condchain.use_case: Rapid iteration/contamination-free eval vs comprehensive multi-domain eval

from_dynamath.generation: Optimized <100ms pipeline vs sophisticated math rendering

positioning_statement: CompViz is explicitly positioned as a complementary tool: MM-CondChain provides comprehensive multi-domain evaluation with conditional reasoning; DynaMath provides mathematical reasoning robustness testing; CompViz provides ultra-fast generation enabling contamination-free per-run evaluation with focus on nested quantification and transitive reasoning. Researchers use all three for comprehensive assessment.
open_source_plan: Immediate release upon announcement: (1) Complete Python generator with modular grammar; (2) Pre-configured difficulty presets; (3) LM Evaluation Harness integration; (4) VLMEvalKit integration; (5) Documented failure modes and mitigations; (6) Query naturalness validation data. Goal: establish community adoption alongside MM-CondChain and DynaMath.
venue_target: NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks Track, CVPR, or ICCV
